hypothesis: Incorporating explicit many-body dispersion interactions (beyond the pairwise DFT-D3 correction applied at inference time) directly into the uMLIP training objective for MOF-relevant chemical environments will yield greater accuracy improvements in bulk modulus and heat capacity predictions than further scaling of training dataset size alone, because the systematic underestimation of long-range collective polarization effects in highly porous, low-density frameworks represents a fundamental limitation of the current pairwise dispersion correction scheme that cannot be compensated by additional non-equilibrium training configurations.
